test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require creating new files (hub-a/b/c page.tsx, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md) and modifying existing files (dynamic-stale-10/60 page.tsx, test file). A stub/no-op implementation would fail because the files don't exist at the base commit. The tests check for specific content that must be deliberately added.

The instruction also requires 'act scopes with includes assertions matching hub content' but tests only check for hub path strings, not act scope usage patterns.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section headers (Pass-to-pass static, Pass-to-pass repo_tests, Fail-to-pass pr_diff code behavior, Fail-to-pass config/instruction), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces source code files and documentation, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction specifies exact file paths to create/modify, exact content patterns (e.g., 'export default function Page()', 'await connection()'), exact navigation patterns to use in the test ('Navigate to /per-page-config/hub-a, hub-b, hub-c instead of using await browser.back()'), and exact documentation content. config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is primarily a code task involving creating new files and modifying existing ones, not a config-edit-only task.
